commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=c16ae561a2c6878876626cbeb79aff91e40bc964)
commands.review_summary:
The PR forwards `BWS_SERVER_URL` from the bundled Bitwarden resolver to the spawned `bws` CLI, updates the Bitwarden secrets docs, and adds resolver subprocess env tests.

PR surface: Tests +87, Docs +3, Other +6. Total +96 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current `main` only passes `passEnv` variables to exec resolvers and the current Bitwarden resolver only forwards `BWS_ACCESS_TOKEN` and `PATH` to `bws`. I did not run a live self-hosted Bitwarden repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor-owned changes and after-fix proof for the documented exec-provider path; automation should not claim real behavior proof for the contributor's setup.

target.title: fix(secrets): forward BWS_SERVER_URL to the bws resolver for self-hosted Bitwarden (fixes #93851)
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes #93851.
- Self-hosted Bitwarden Secrets Manager users hit `401 Unauthorized`: the bundled resolver `scripts/secrets/openclaw-bws-resolver.mjs` spawned the `bws` CLI with a hardcoded child env (`BWS_ACCESS_TOKEN`, `PATH`) only, so `BWS_SERVER_URL` was dropped even after adding it to the provider `passEnv`.
- The resolver now forwards `BWS_SERVER_URL` to the `bws` child process when it is present, so `bws` targets the configured self-hosted server instead of the public default.
- Intentionally unchanged: the core exec secrets provider keeps its minimal-by-default child env; env is still forwarded explicitly. No core runtime, config, or schema changes.

## Why not change the core exec provider default

A previous version of this PR made the core exec provider inherit the full parent env by default. That approach was dropped because:

1. It violates the documented contract — `docs/gateway/secrets.md`: "exec child environment is minimal by default; pass required variables explicitly with `passEnv`" — and conflicts with the sibling `src/security/install-policy.ts` posture (`does not inherit PATH unless passEnv includes it`).
2. It would not even fix the bug. Even with the full env handed to the resolver, the bundled resolver re-spawns `bws` with its own hardcoded env subset, so `BWS_SERVER_URL` was still dropped. The real owner of the drop is the resolver script, which is what this PR fixes.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: bundled `bws` resolver (`scripts/secrets/openclaw-bws-resolver.mjs`) dropped `BWS_SERVER_URL` when spawning the `bws` CLI, so self-hosted Bitwarden returned 401 Unauthorized.
Real environment tested: Linux, Node v22.22.0; ran the actual shipped resolver directly with `node` against a stand-in `bws` placed on PATH that records the environment handed to it.
Exact steps or command run after this patch:
`echo '{"protocolVersion":1,"ids":["openclaw/providers/openai/apiKey"]}' | BWS_ACCESS_TOKEN=demo-token BWS_BIN=./bws BWS_SERVER_URL=https://pass.example.com node scripts/secrets/openclaw-bws-resolver.mjs`
Evidence after fix:

```
=========== RUN 1: self-hosted (BWS_SERVER_URL set) ===========
{"protocolVersion":1,"values":{"openclaw/providers/openai/apiKey":"sk-live-demo-value"},"errors":{}}
--- env captured by the bws child ---
bws child saw BWS_SERVER_URL=https://pass.example.com
=========== RUN 2: SaaS default (BWS_SERVER_URL unset) ===========
{"protocolVersion":1,"values":{"openclaw/providers/openai/apiKey":"sk-live-demo-value"},"errors":{}}
--- env captured by the bws child ---
bws child saw BWS_SERVER_URL=(unset)
```

Observed result after fix: with `BWS_SERVER_URL` set in the parent environment, the `node`-spawned `bws` child received `BWS_SERVER_URL=https://pass.example.com` and the resolver returned the requested secret; with it unset, the child env stays minimal (`(unset)`), preserving the documented minimal-env contract.
What was not tested: a live self-hosted Bitwarden Secrets Manager server with a real `bws` binary and credentials.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/openclaw-bws-resolver.test.ts` → 2 passed (regression: forwards `BWS_SERVER_URL` when set, omits it when absent)
- `npx oxfmt --check scripts/secrets/openclaw-bws-resolver.mjs test/scripts/openclaw-bws-resolver.test.ts` → clean

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? Yes — self-hosted `bws` resolvers now receive `BWS_SERVER_URL`.
Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No core config/schema change; users still add `BWS_SERVER_URL` to `passEnv` as documented.
Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? Minimal — one named parent env var is forwarded to the resolver-spawned `bws` child only when set; the core minimal-env contract is unchanged.
Highest-risk area: secrets resolver subprocess env.
Mitigation: forward only the named variable when present; no full parent-env inheritance; regression coverage for both present and absent cases.

## Current review state

Next action: maintainer review.
Waiting on: review.
Bot comments addressed: replaced the prior core-change approach with the minimal owner-module fix; refreshed real behavior proof with direct resolver run output.
target.author: pick-cat
